contract: {rating, notes} — exactly two keys, rating int 0-100, notes non-empty, one bare JSON object
comment: schema_cases run with NO model (pure Python via chaos_spec.schema_ok) and MUST pass exactly as marked — the deterministic forcing function for output shape. band_cases are the labeled held-out examples; the runner checks the reference oracle lands in band (a known-good baseline) and that an empty emission scores 0.0 (cannot saturate). A live agent's recorded outputs can be checked the same way once a run exists. Prose is never compared.

output.notes: An injected dependency or instance failure is contained, degrades gracefully, and recovers automatically; no change needed.

Inject a recommender outage: recommender.fetch has no timeout and no fallback in the request path, so the hang takes the whole home page down with no steady state held and no recovery; wrap it with a 200ms timeout and a circuit breaker whose fallback serves cached recommendations so the failure is contained and self-heals to reach 100.

Inject an upstream outage: the unbounded while-True retry with no backoff or jitter hammers the failing dependency into a retry storm and never returns, a stuck state with no cap; add a bounded retry (max attempts) with exponential backoff and jitter plus a circuit breaker and fallback to fix
